annotations.content: For all the inhabitants of this seasonal forest,
the long, cold wait is nearly over.

The vultures have left behind
plenty of good meat,
Spring in a deciduous woodland is special.

The mother works to open the hide, and
make feeding a little easier for her cub.
With no leaves overhead, the rays of the sun strike the forest floor directly,
and their warmth rouses plants from their winter sleep.
The ground living plants are in a hurry.

There are only 40 Amur leopards
left in the wild,
Before long, the trees above will come into leaf and steal their light.
and that number is still falling.

The harshness of the winter here
hinders their increasing numbers.

It takes one of these females longer
to raise her young to independence ...
Their flowers decorate the forest floor
as they advertise their sweet nectar to the newly emerged insects.
than it does a leopard in Africa.

If the mother can sustain her cub
for a few more weeks,

spring will bring an increase in
prey and her task will lighten.
The spring blooms of the deciduous woodlands have no equivalent
in either the great conifer forests, or the tropical jungles.

For all the inhabitants
of this seasonal forest,
the long, cold wait is nearly over.
Within a matter of weeks, the canopy has closed, and only a few wheeling shafts of light penetrate the woodland.

Spring in a deciduous woodland
is special.
In the treetops, the broad leaves rapidly expand to their full size to make the most of summer while it lasts.

With no leaves overhead, the rays of the
sun strike the forest floor directly,

and their warmth rouses plants
from their winter sleep.
Then, after a few months, the days begin to shorten again, and the trees must shut down and shed their leaves in preparation for the cold dark time ahead.

The ground living plants
are in a hurry.

Before long, the trees above will
come into leaf and steal their light.
Great tracts of North America flush red as the season progresses.
The effect is so spectacular and so extensive that it can be seen from space.

Their flowers decorate
the forest floor ...

as they advertise their sweet nectar
to the newly emerged insects.
The threat of winter frost is not the only reason for trees to shed leaves.

The spring blooms of the deciduous
woodlands have no equivalent ...
